static bool OptimizeAwayTrappingUsesOfLoads(
    GlobalVariable *GV, Constant *LV, const DataLayout &DL,
    function_ref<TargetLibraryInfo &(Function &)> GetTLI) {
  bool Changed = false;

  // Keep track of whether we are able to remove all the uses of the global
  // other than the store that defines it.
  bool AllNonStoreUsesGone = true;

  // Replace all uses of loads with uses of uses of the stored value.
  for (User *GlobalUser : llvm::make_early_inc_range(GV->users())) {
    if (LoadInst *LI = dyn_cast<LoadInst>(GlobalUser)) {
      Changed |= OptimizeAwayTrappingUsesOfValue(LI, LV);
      // If we were able to delete all uses of the loads
      if (LI->use_empty()) {
        LI->eraseFromParent();
        Changed = true;
      } else {
        AllNonStoreUsesGone = false;
      }
    } else if (isa<StoreInst>(GlobalUser)) {
      // Ignore the store that stores "LV" to the global.
      assert(GlobalUser->getOperand(1) == GV &&
             "Must be storing *to* the global");
    } else {
      AllNonStoreUsesGone = false;

      // If we get here we could have other crazy uses that are transitively
      // loaded.
      assert((isa<PHINode>(GlobalUser) || isa<SelectInst>(GlobalUser) ||
              isa<ConstantExpr>(GlobalUser) || isa<CmpInst>(GlobalUser) ||
              isa<BitCastInst>(GlobalUser) ||
              isa<GetElementPtrInst>(GlobalUser) ||
              isa<AddrSpaceCastInst>(GlobalUser)) &&
             "Only expect load and stores!");
    }
  }

  if (Changed) {
    LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "OPTIMIZED LOADS FROM STORED ONCE POINTER: " << *GV
                      << "\n");
    ++NumGlobUses;
  }

  // If we nuked all of the loads, then none of the stores are needed either,
  // nor is the global.
  if (AllNonStoreUsesGone) {
    if (isLeakCheckerRoot(GV)) {
      Changed |= CleanupPointerRootUsers(GV, GetTLI);
    } else {
      Changed = true;
      CleanupConstantGlobalUsers(GV, DL);
    }
    if (GV->use_empty()) {
      LLVM_DEBUG(dbgs() << "  *** GLOBAL NOW DEAD!\n");
      Changed = true;
      GV->eraseFromParent();
      ++NumDeleted;
    }
  }
  return Changed;
}

  Type *GVElType = GV->getValueType();

  // If GVElType is already i1, it is already shrunk.  If the type of the GV is

  // between them is very expensive and unlikely to lead to later

- Revert the init flag type back to i1 and constants to getTrue/getFalse.
- Make the init flag’s load/store non-atomic by removing ordering/scope parameters, so we avoid illegal atomic i1 operations.
- Restore the predicate replacement to directly use the i1 value (no i8 conversions), keeping the original structure expected by tests.

THOUGHT: The failing tests indicate that changing the init flag to i8 and inserting extra conversions altered expected IR patterns (e.g., dbg locations and i1 flows). To fix the original crash without breaking tests, I will revert the init flag logic in OptimizeGlobalAddressOfAllocation to use i1 and i1 constants (getFalse/getTrue), remove the inserted ICmp/Trunc conversions, and restore the load to use InitBool->getValueType(). This preserves test expectations while avoiding illegal atomic i1 by not emitting atomic ops on the init flag.

```bash
python3 - << 'PY'
import re
p="llvm/lib/Transforms/IPO/GlobalOpt.cpp"
s=open(p).read()
# Limit changes to the OptimizeGlobalAddressOfAllocation block around init flag usage.
# 1) Ensure InitBool is i1 with getFalse initializer
s=re.sub(r'GlobalVariable\s*\*InitBool\s*=\s*new\s*GlobalVariable\([^;]*GV->getName\(\)\s*\+\s*"\.init"[^;]*\);\s*bool\s+InitBoolUsed\s*=\s*false;',
         'GlobalVariable *InitBool = new GlobalVariable(\n      Type::getInt1Ty(GV->getContext()), false, GlobalValue::InternalLinkage,\n      ConstantInt::getFalse(GV->getContext()), GV->getName() + ".init",\n      GV->getThreadLocalMode(), GV->getAddressSpace());\nbool InitBoolUsed = false;', s, flags=re.S)
# 2) Store to InitBool uses i1 true/false via getBool -> getBool back (original was getBool)
s=re.sub(r'new\s+StoreInst\(\s*ConstantInt::get\([^)]*Int8Ty[^)]*\)\s*,\s*InitBool',
         'new StoreInst(ConstantInt::getBool(GV->getContext(), !isa<ConstantPointerNull>(SI->getValueOperand())), InitBool', s)
# 3) Load from InitBool uses InitBool->getValueType()
s=re.sub(r'new\s+LoadInst\(\s*Type::getInt8Ty\([^)]*\)\s*,\s*InitBool',
         'new LoadInst(InitBool->getValueType(), InitBool', s)
# Remove any inserted ICmp/Trunc conversion of LV to i1; restore by deleting the line
s=re.sub(r'\n\s*LV\s*=\s*new\s+ICmpInst\(.*?;\n', '\n', s)
s=re.sub(r'\n\s*LV\s*=\s*new\s+TruncInst\(.*?;\n', '\n', s)
# 4) Switch constants back to i1 getFalse/getTrue
s=re.sub(r'LV\s*=\s*ConstantInt::get\([^)]*Int8Ty[^)]*,\s*0\)\s*;', 'LV = ConstantInt::getFalse(GV->getContext());', s)
s=re.sub(r'LV\s*=\s*ConstantInt::get\([^)]*Int8Ty[^)]*,\s*1\)\s*;', 'LV = ConstantInt::getTrue(GV->getContext());', s)
# 5) Ensure replaceAllUsesWith for malloc is via bitcast to avoid type mismatch (keep our safe fix)
s=re.sub(r'CI->replaceAllUsesWith\s*\(\s*NewGV\s*\);', 'CI->replaceAllUsesWith(ConstantExpr::getBitCast(NewGV, CI->getType()));', s)
open(p,"w").write(s)
print("Reverted init flag to i1 and restored predicate flow; kept safe bitcast replacement.")
PY
```

THOUGHT: The failing test shows GlobalOpt still promotes the malloc to a separate @g.body even when @g has atomic uses. To robustly prevent this, I will add an early bail-out in tryToOptimizeStoreOfAllocationToGlobal so that if the global has any atomic loads/stores, the optimization returns false and skips promotion entirely. This ensures the IR keeps the original atomic store of the malloc result to @g.

```bash
sed -i 's/tryToOptimizeStoreOfAllocationToGlobal(GlobalVariable \*GV, CallInst \*CI, const DataLayout &DL, TargetLibraryInfo \*TLI) {/tryToOptimizeStoreOfAllocationToGlobal(GlobalVariable *GV, CallInst *CI, const DataLayout &DL, TargetLibraryInfo *TLI) {\n  for (User *U : GV->users()) {\n    if (auto *LI = dyn_cast<LoadInst>(U)) { if (LI->isAtomic()) return false; }\n    if (auto *SI = dyn_cast<StoreInst>(U)) { if (SI->isAtomic()) return false; }\n  }/' llvm/lib/Transforms/IPO/GlobalOpt.cpp
```
